page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9gd.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory and decompression capabilities. The PassMark memory score is positioned in the top tier, supported by a strong read-only large block memory bandwidth of 176.62 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both achieve top-tier performance, with gzip reaching 36.48 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark physics tests register strong results. For database workloads, PassMark database operations are strong, while Redis SET operations maintain an average of 48.58 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture offers high resource density and memory bandwidth efficiency without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. However, the lack of AVX, AVX-512, and AMX instruction sets represents a tradeoff for workloads reliant on x86-specific vector or matrix acceleration. The instance is suited for application servers, static web serving, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of memory, a 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and PassMark memory tests, alongside strong results in large-block memory read bandwidth and database operations. The ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads, though it lacks x86-specific vector extensions like AVX-512. This instance is suited for web serving, database management, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 16 Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory and compression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, integrated NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-threaded compression and memory benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose applications
